campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also heard from the public on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing under Government Code Section 3502
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on council reports

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council passed a resolution stating the city's intent to identify certain properties and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026. At that hearing, residents will have a chance to object to the proposal before the council makes a final decision.
keyTopics: Resolution approved declaring city's intent to identify specific properties
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can file protests and voice concerns at the June hearing
Final approval decision expected after public hearing

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Town Council approved routine meeting minutes, selected a new Youth Commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election for three open seats. They also approved several operational agreements including a new software contract with Accela and authorized the Town Manager to finalize deals for building inspection services and a new development project map.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 municipal election scheduled for three Town Council seats
New Youth Commissioner appointment confirmed
Five-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. for town operations
Final map approval for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
Building Division plan check service agreements authorized

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize upcoming agendas for June 16 and June 23, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked waiver requirements for agenda items and documents.
keyTopics: Final agenda preparation for June 16, 2026 meeting
Draft agenda planning for June 23, 2026 meeting
Community and Economic Development Committee work plan review

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 26, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View city leaders discussed updates to the Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan and reviewed the city's plan to reduce carbon emissions over the next five years. The council also recognized Bike Month with a proclamation.
keyTopics: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan adoption
City decarbonization goals and five-year action plan
Bike Month proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on June 2, 2026, with two parts: a closed-door session followed by a public special order of the day meeting. The specific topics discussed in the closed session were not disclosed to the public, as is typical for confidential council discussions.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting
Special order of the day items

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 10, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to review meeting minutes from April and discuss upcoming summer activities for the community. The council covered plans for a Teen Resource Fair, volunteer opportunities for recreation events, and presented information about summer events happening in the city.
keyTopics: Summer events programming and calendar
Teen Resource Fair planning
Recruitment of volunteers for recreation events
Approval of April 2026 meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 9, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 9, 2026, available both in-person and online. The city is accepting public comments from residents who want to weigh in on council decisions.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in-person or join via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents unable to attend
Public comment submission through eComments system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes and reviewing the city's housing and community development plans for 2025-2026. The council also discussed changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed requests from individual council members for future agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Changes to the council vacancy filling process
New workplan requests from council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council considered raising your monthly storm drain and surface water fee by 3% to $18.14 per household starting in fiscal year 2027, based on inflation adjustments. The council also discussed state rules limiting building electrification efforts and whether the city should provide additional subsidies to help residents switch to electric systems.
keyTopics: Storm water drainage fee increase of 3% (about 50 cents more per month)
State-imposed caps on electrification programs
Potential new city subsidies for electric heating and appliance conversions
